source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Seadrill West Neptune Slip and Cut Drill Line Injury

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Keathley Canyon Block 785

actions_taken: Employee evacuated to shore by helicopter on 8 March 2024
Employee transported to onshore physician for medical evaluation
BSEE onsite investigation conducted on 28 March 2024
G-110 Incident of Non-Compliance issued to LLOG
event.summary: On 7 March 2024 at 1500 hours, during slip and cut drill line operations on the Seadrill West Neptune drillship, a 2-inch diameter drill line (approximately 19 feet long, 148 pounds) slipped from a nylon web sling being used with an air tugger and fell freely to the rig floor. A Seadrill employee attempting to exit the area tripped over coiled drill line and fell, and was subsequently struck by the falling drill line on his hardhat, middle back, and left shoulder. The employee sustained a torn labrum and shoulder strain requiring 10-12 weeks of physical therapy.
key_phrases: improper use of nylon web sling
lack of instructions in Permit to Work SOM
tying off drill line to cat head

hazards.name: Abnormal twist formation in drill line during spooling
Personnel in line of fire during lifting operations

threats.name: Improper rigging of suspended load with nylon web sling
Loss of grip/bite of nylon web sling on drill line
Deviation from standard slip and cut procedures
Inadequate communication of lifting method to supervisor
Personnel positioned in drop zone during lowering operation

consequences.name: Lost time injury requiring evacuation and medical treatment

controls.name: Permit to Work Standard Operating Manual for Slip & Cut Drill Line
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure safe execution of slip and cut operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent deviation from safe procedures

supporting_text: the Permit to Work Standard Operation Manual for slip and cut drill line lacked instructions
Job procedures not followed. Poor pre-job task and the Permit to Work Standard Operation Manual for slip and cut drill line lacked instructions.

controls.name: Supervision of slip and cut drill line operations
controls.barrier_role: Supervisory oversight to ensure procedures are followed and safe practices maintained
controls.lod_basis: Direct supervision to prevent deviations and unsafe practices

supporting_text: Inadequate supervision. The Supervisor for the slip and cut drill line operations was not aware of the Seadrill Crew's plan of using a nylon web sling.

controls.name: Communication of lifting method and operational plan
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all personnel understand the planned lifting operation and hazards
controls.lod_basis: Clear communication prevents misunderstandings and unsafe deviations

supporting_text: The Derrickman informed the Driller that the crew will be lifting the drill line, but he failed to communicate how the drill line was going to be lifted.

Inadequate job instructions provided. The Driller was informed by the Derrickman about that the Seadrill Crew's decision to lift the 2-inch drill line, but he failed to communicate to the Driller how this operation was going to be performed.

controls.name: Proper rigging and use of nylon web sling for suspended loads
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure suspended load is properly secured and will not slip
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to prevent load slip if procedures are deviated

supporting_text: The improper use a nylon web sling that lost its bite allowed the 2-inch diameter drill line to slip through the sling and fall freely to the rig floor injuring a Seadrill employee.

A nylon web sling was then placed about 4 feet from the end of the drill line. The Air Tugger Operator lifted the drill line about 20 to 25 feet and the drill line righted the abnormal twist. The Air Tugger Operator started to lower the drill line when it slipped from the nylon web sling and fell freely to the rig floor.
improper use of a nylon web sling that allowed the drill line to slip out

controls.name: Management of change procedures for deviation from standard slip and cut operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to evaluate and approve deviations from standard procedures
controls.lod_basis: Prevent unauthorized deviations that could introduce new hazards

supporting_text: Inadequate management of change procedures. The Seadrill Crew deviated from the normal procedures for slip and cut drill line by tying of the drill line to the Cat Head.
the crew deviated from the normal procedures for slip and cut drill line

controls.name: Personnel positioning and exclusion from line of fire during lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from being in drop zone during suspended load operations
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to prevent injury if load is dropped

supporting_text: A Seadrill employee, situated in the line of fire attempted to move out of the way. As he was exiting the area, he tripped over the coiled drill line and fell on his right shoulder and face on the rig floor.
the Seadrill employee who had fallen to the rig floor on his right shoulder when he was exiting the cone of exposure

controls.name: Air tugger operation and load control procedures
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper operation of air tugger to maintain control of suspended load
controls.lod_basis: Equipment control to prevent uncontrolled lowering of load

supporting_text: The Air Tugger Operator started to lower the drill line when it slipped from the nylon web sling and fell freely to the rig floor.
the nylon web sling lost its bite that allowed 19 feet of the 2-inch diameter drill line weighing 148 pounds to fall freely to the rig floor

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
